title: tanacetum vahlii var. subflosculosum
intro: Tanacetum vahlii var. subflosculosum, also known as the Himalayan pyrethrum, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family, characterized by its small flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Tanacetum vahlii var. subflosculosum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
